overview.name: BMAD Protocol Enforcer
overview.description: Mechanical enforcement of the Architect↔Builder A2A discipline. PhaseGates block execution until approved. ArchitectHandoff structures spec delivery. BuilderBlocker structures blocker reporting. PAULIWHEEL loop stages enforced during execution.
overview.philosophy: DISCIPLINE IS CODE. If it's not enforced mechanically, it won't run under pressure.

file_structure.archonx/protocols/__init__.py: Package init, exports BMAdProtocol, PhaseGate, ArchitectHandoff, BuilderBlocker
file_structure.archonx/protocols/bmad.py: Main BMAD enforcer — all classes in one file for simplicity
file_structure.archonx/protocols/phases.py: Phase definitions, valid transitions, gate conditions

PHASE_0.name: PREPARATION
PHASE_0.description: Scan codebase, identify gaps, produce gap analysis
PHASE_0.exit_condition: gap analysis produced + explicit APPROVED: START PHASE_1 received

PHASE_1.name: ARCHITECTURE
PHASE_1.description: Architect produces specs, Builder reviews, clarifies
PHASE_1.exit_condition: all 6 specs produced + APPROVED: START PHASE_2

PHASE_2.name: IMPLEMENTATION
PHASE_2.description: Builder implements specs with zero human loop
PHASE_2.exit_condition: all implementations complete + all tests green + APPROVED: START PHASE_3

PHASE_3.name: DEPLOYMENT
PHASE_3.description: Final deployment — human provides Vercel Project IDs
PHASE_3.entry_condition: APPROVED: START PHASE_3 + human provides Vercel Project IDs
PHASE_3.exit_condition: live Vercel deployment + smoke tests pass

PhaseGate.description: Blocks execution at phase boundaries. Must be explicitly unlocked by Architect.

fields.unlock_token: str | None — the explicit approval phrase required
fields.required_token: str — exact phrase that unlocks (e.g. 'APPROVED: START PHASE_1')
fields.unlocked_by: str | None — who provided the approval

PhaseGate.behavior: If gate is locked and builder tries to execute phase, raise PhaseGateLocked exception with clear message

ArchitectHandoff.description: Structured spec delivery from Architect to Builder.

fields.phase: BMAdPhase — which phase this handoff governs
fields.spec_files: list[str] — paths to JSON spec files
fields.approval_phrase: str — exact phrase Builder must emit to proceed

BuilderBlocker.description: Structured blocker report from Builder to Architect.

fields.description: str — detailed description of the blocker

BMAdState.description: Persisted state across sessions. Survives process restarts.
BMAdState.persistence: .archonx/bmad_state.json — written atomically on every state change

BMAdProtocol.description: Main enforcement class. Kernel holds one instance. All phase transitions go through this.

methods.check_phase_allowed(target_phase: BMAdPh: Raises PhaseGateLocked if gate to target_phase is locked
methods.advance_phase(approval_token: str) -> BM: Check token matches required_token for next gate, unlock, return new phase
methods.register_blocker(blocker: BuilderBlocker: Save blocker to state, return blocker_id
methods.record_handoff(handoff: ArchitectHandoff: Record spec handoff, mark gate ready for unlock
methods.export_status() -> dict: Full JSON status report (current phase, gates, active blockers, history)

pauliwheel_integration.description: PAULIWHEEL loop stages are enforced WITHIN Phase 2 implementation tasks
pauliwheel_integration.bmad_requirement: Every PHASE_2 implementation bead MUST include bead_id + go through PAULIWHEEL stages. BeadExecutor in agents/lightning/bead_executor.py already implements this — Hermes, Popebot, Chess Theater builders MUST use BeadExecutor for their work.

kernel_integration.execute_task_guard: At top of execute_task(): self.bmad.check_phase_allowed(BMAdPhase.PHASE_2) (raises if not in active phase)
kernel_integration.phase_advance_hook: kernel.advance_bmad_phase(token: str) -> called when Architect provides approval phrase
kernel_integration.blocker_hook: kernel.report_blocker(component, description, request) -> saves BuilderBlocker to state

approval_tokens.note: These EXACT strings must be present in the approval message to unlock the gate. Case-sensitive.

exception_classes.PhaseGateLocked: Raised when Builder attempts to execute in a locked phase. Message includes: current phase, required token to unlock.
exception_classes.UnknownPhaseTransition: Raised when approval token doesn't match any known gate.
exception_classes.DuplicateBlocker: Raised when same component+description blocker already exists (prevent spam).

state_file.atomic_write: Write to .archonx/bmad_state.json.tmp then rename to avoid corruption

test_requirements: test_gate_blocks_wrong_phase: builder tries to execute phase 2 while in phase 1 → PhaseGateLocked raised
test_correct_token_advances_phase: 'APPROVED: START PHASE_2' → phase advances to PHASE_2
test_wrong_token_rejected: wrong approval phrase → phase stays locked
test_blocker_registered_and_resolved: BuilderBlocker saved and resolved in state
test_state_persists_across_reload: save state, reload BMAdProtocol, verify same state
builder_notes: State file lives in .archonx/ — this directory already exists (archonx/reportback.json is there)
BMAD enforcer should be LIGHTWEIGHT — it's governance, not business logic. Keep bmad.py under 300 lines.
Phase gate checking is synchronous (no async needed — it's just a state check)
Initial state when first deployed: current_phase = PHASE_1 (we are in Phase 1 now)
The approval tokens are case-sensitive exact matches — no fuzzy matching
BuilderBlocker must be LOGGED to ops/reports/ as well as state file (for Architect visibility)
